mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment and expanding payroll employment indicate continuing household income growth, supporting earnings expectations and risk appetite, so the S&P 500 earns positive forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.8 and PAYEMS was 150816 as of the latest available snapshot
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if periods with UNRATE below 4.0 and positive PAYEMS 3-month growth do not produce higher 20-day SPX returns than the full-sample baseline, or if hit rate is below 50% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 2.25 percent during a mature expansion tightens discount rates and lowers equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 2.41 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases or if mean triggered return is not below the unconditional mean at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in unemployment larger than 0.3 percentage points signals deterioration in labor demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: labor-market deterioration after an expansion peak
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not have lower 60-day SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the negative return hit rate is below 55% in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production identifies weakening factory output and operating leverage, reducing expected earnings growth and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 110.475 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO contraction triggers have average 60-day SPX returns equal to or above unconditional returns, or if Rank IC is above -0.03 in fewer than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1200 thousand units signal weak interest-sensitive demand and slower construction-linked spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1162.0 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-below-1200 observations deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or show no negative return differential versus baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate resilient household demand and near-term revenue momentum for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days after the signal is observed.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 505969.0 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS monthly-change triggers fail to raise the 5-day SPX hit rate above 52% or if mean triggered return is not positive in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is high and CPI momentum is not accelerating, policy is restrictive relative to inflation pressure, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 2.41 and CPIAUCSL was 254.148 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger does not underperform either single-series trigger, or if triggered 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: When payrolls keep expanding while real GDP growth slows, the profit share is squeezed by labor costs against softer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter growth decelerates while PAYEMS 3-month growth remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 18765.256 and PAYEMS was 150816.0 as of the latest available snapshot
failure_condition: Reject if the joint decelerating-GDP and positive-payroll trigger has nonnegative average 60-day SPX excess returns versus baseline or if negative-return hit rate is below 55%.
